Patch Set 1:

what about rsync case? lfs migrate is safe, but fallback to rsync is not safe from in-use file, right?

Patch Set 1:

> what about rsync case? lfs migrate is safe, but fallback to rsync
 > is not safe from in-use file, right?

True...  The question is whether the message/prompt should only be printed when the rsync fallback is needed?  Having the prompt at the beginning is annoying for the common case where rsync is not needed, but prompting in the middle can be confusing for users.

Patch Set 3:

> what about rsync case? lfs migrate is safe, but fallback to rsync
 > is not safe from in-use file, right?

The automatic fallback to rsync mode was undesirable for other reasons and has been disabled by default in this version of the patch.  That removes the need to have the "-y" prompt message under normal usage, and it now only appears when --rsync is explicitly used.

parents.subject: LU-6142 ldlm: use list_for_each_entry in ldlm_resource.c

commit.subject: LU-13475 utils: disable lfs_migrate rsync and warning
commit.message:
LU-13475 utils: disable lfs_migrate rsync and warning

The --rsync option is no longer enabled by default for fallback if
'lfs migrate' fails for some reason, and is mandatory for rsync usage.
The warning message and "-y" option of lfs_migrate is no longer needed
if rsync is not used, and is only shown if --rsync is used.

Remove the LFS_MIGRATE_RSYNC_MODE variable that was used for tests
and instead pass the "--rsync" option directly when needed.

Test-Parameters: trivial
Signed-off-by: Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: I70b70d969f2dc8b4836c6c7692e6a73a0e2540e5
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/40614
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Jian Yu <yujian@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Vitaliy Kuznetsov <vkuznetsov@ddn.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
